CVX.reasoning: CVX has completed the relief bounce the wiki predicted: short entry was flagged at $192 on the prior run (Apr 11), and price has now reached exactly $192.22 intraday. The consolidation range was $183.67 (pivot low, 24 hourly tests) to $188.37 (pivot high zone, 16 tests); price bounced out of that range to $192 — the wiki's defined short entry and resistance level. Breakout direction read: BEARISH. The daily trend remains bearish — ADX 32.17 (strong) with -DI (28.59) > +DI (21.56), MACD histogram negative (-0.53), and daily OBV in a downtrend that is diverging from price (bearish divergence). Volume is BELOW average (relative volume 0.92x on daily, 0.82x on hourly) — no bullish volume confirmation for a sustained upside breakout. Hourly RSI at 70.25 is overbought with a bearish RSI divergence. The hourly BB pct_b is 1.04 (price above upper band), signaling overextension on the bounce. Daily BB width of 0.14 is not compressed (no squeeze for a bull breakout). The measured-move target for the bear side: range height from $188.37 resistance to $183.67 support = $4.70; projecting below $183.67 support gives ~$178 — consistent with the wiki's $178 target and the $177.74 recent low. Stop: $199 per wiki invalidation level, which is also the Fib 61.8% retracement at $191.86 + buffer. Entering short at the current bounce level of $192; stop $199 (2% above wiki invalidation); target $178 (first major support). Risk-reward: $7 risk vs $14 reward = 2.0:1. Confidence held at 62 (not higher) due to lack of volume confirmation on the breakdown leg — the bear case needs a close below $183.67 support to confirm the downside breakout.

WMT.reasoning: WMT remains in the identical no-setup condition documented across both prior runs (Apr 11 and Apr 15). The wiki rule is explicit: require a daily close above $129 on 1.5x+ volume before any bull entry. Today's daily close is $127.59 — still below the $128.49 resistance (22 tests) and well below the $129 breakout trigger. Volume is 0.98x average — not elevated. ADX at 14.72 is far below the 25 threshold required to confirm any directional trend — this is a choppy, non-directional environment. Consolidation range is defined by support at $121.37 (14 tests) and resistance at $128.49-$129.00 (22-30 tests) — a $7.52 range that has been in place. The measured-move target if $129 breaks would be ~$136.50 (range height added to breakout level), consistent with the Fib extension at $136.39. However, the breakout has NOT occurred: recent price action shows a failed breakout — WMT hit $132.46 on Apr 23 but has pulled back sharply to $127.59 over the past 2 sessions (down from the high). Hourly indicators are bearish: ADX 26.04 with -DI (27.82) > +DI (18.24), hourly RSI 39.2, MACD histogram negative (-0.35). Hourly BB width at 0.0487 shows some compression, but this is on the downside leg, not a coiling before an upside break. OBV on daily is trending up and not diverging — the one positive — but insufficient alone. No squeeze is firing. No consolidation base forming. The prior high-volume pivot at $129.77 (23 hourly tests, volume confirmed) is still intact overhead. Wait for a clean daily close above $129-$129.77 on 1.5x+ volume, or re-evaluate after May 21 Q1 FY2027 earnings.
